question_title: Which will be higher in the first official ISM reports for March 2026: the Manufacturing PMI or the Services PMI?
summary:
Services PMI is strongly favored to be higher than Manufacturing PMI in the March 2026 ISM reports. The historical base rate is overwhelming: Services PMI has exceeded Manufacturing PMI every single month for 26+ consecutive months (all of 2024-2026). In February 2026, the gap was 3.7 points (Services 56.1 vs Manufacturing 52.4).

The main counter-signal is the S&P Global Flash PMI for March, which shows Manufacturing (52.4) exceeding Services (51.1). However, ISM and S&P Global surveys diverge significantly: ISM Services runs ~4.4 points above S&P Global Services, while ISM Manufacturing runs only ~0.8 above. Scenario analysis shows Manufacturing would need ISM to amplify S&P Global changes by 2.6x to overcome the gap — requiring Manufacturing up ~2.1 AND Services down ~1.6 simultaneously. Consensus forecasts for ISM Manufacturing March are ~52.2, still well below any plausible ISM Services reading. Equal probability is negligible since both indices are reported to one decimal place.

factors.description: Historical base rate: Services PMI has been consistently higher than Manufacturing PMI for 26+ consecutive months through Feb 2026. Manufacturing was in contraction for most of 2022-2025, only recently crossing above 50.
February 2026 gap: ISM Services PMI (56.1) was 3.7 points above Manufacturing PMI (52.4). This is a very large gap to overcome in a single month.
S&P Global Flash PMI for March 2026 shows Manufacturing (52.4) > Services (51.1), suggesting directional convergence in manufacturing's favor. This is the strongest counter-signal available.

ISM surveys diverge significantly from S&P Global: ISM Services runs ~4.4 points above S&P Global Services, while ISM Manufacturing runs only ~0.8 above. This structural premium insulates the ISM gap from S&P Global reversals.
Consensus forecast for March ISM Manufacturing PMI is ~52.2, well below any plausible ISM Services reading given recent trajectory.
Equal requires exact match to one decimal place between two independently surveyed indices — extremely unlikely given typical month-to-month variation.
Middle East war disruptions and tariff front-loading could temporarily boost manufacturing orders while hurting services sentiment, though the magnitude needed for a full reversal is very large.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Services PMI higher (85%) | Manufacturing PMI higher (13%) | Equal (2%)**

Key reasoning:

- **Services dominance is the strong base case.** ISM Services PMI has been higher than Manufacturing PMI for 26+ consecutive months. The February gap was 3.7 points (56.1 vs 52.4).

- **S&P Global flash data is the main uncertainty.** The March flash shows Manufacturing (52.4) > Services (51.1), a notable reversal. But ISM and S&P Global diverge structurally — ISM Services runs ~4.4 points above its S&P Global counterpart, while Manufacturing runs only ~0.8 above.

- **Crossover requires extreme moves.** Manufacturing would need to rise ~2.1 points AND Services would need to fall ~1.6 points in a single month for Manufacturing to overtake — a combined swing of 3.7 points that would be historically exceptional.

- **Equal is near-impossible** at ~2%, as matching two independently surveyed indices to one decimal place is extremely unlikely.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option Manufacturing PMI higher if the first official March 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI is greater than the first official March 2026 ISM Services PMI. This question resolves to the option Services PMI higher if the first official March 2026 ISM Services PMI is greater than the first official March 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI. This question resolves to the option Equal if the two headline values are identical. The primary source is ISM’s PMI reports hub at https://www.ismworld.org/supply-management-news-and-reports/reports/ism-pmi-reports/
fine_print: Use the headline PMI values for March 2026 only, not subindexes. If ISM displays the values to one decimal place, compare the published one-decimal values. Candidate Sources: ISM release calendar https://www.ismworld.org/supply-management-news-and-reports/reports/rob-report-calendar/ ; ISM Manufacturing PMI March release page when posted under the same report hub
